what_it_does: This file is an npm package manifest that declares a private ES module named company-channel at version 0.1.0 and lists @modelcontextprotocol/sdk as its only runtime dependency.
observations: The package is marked private and uses type: module.
There are no scripts, devDependencies, repository, or engine fields.
The version uses a caret range.
standouts: Dependency on the Model Context Protocol SDK.
concerns: Manifest is extremely minimal: no scripts, devDependencies, engines, or repository metadata.
The caret range allows automatic minor/patch updates, which may introduce unexpected SDK behavior.
notes: A bare-bones package.json for a private MCP-related channel module; it tells us the intended runtime boundary but not how the SDK is used.
questions: What role does this channel play in the broader MCP architecture (client, server, or both)?
Why is the package private, and is it consumed locally or published internally?
Are there no build, test, or start scripts by design?
intention_signals: It appears to be setting up a private, ES-module-based package whose purpose is to interact with the Model Context Protocol via the official SDK.
contribution: It contributes the package identity, module-type boundary, and the explicit MCP SDK dependency anchor for the channels subsystem.
summary_for_embedding: company/channels/package.json declares a private ES module package named company-channel v0.1.0 with a single dependency on @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.29.0.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to establish a private, ES-module channel package that depends on the Model Context Protocol SDK.
